title: Surface Areas and Volumes of Spheres

summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the surface area formula for a sphere?

The surface area of a sphere is SA = 4πr². Substitute the radius and calculate. The other options — 'S = 2πr²', 'S = πr²', and 'S = 3πr²' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the volume formula for a sphere?

The volume of a sphere is V = (4/3)πr³. Substitute the radius and calculate. The other options — 'V = 4πr²', 'V = (2/3)πr³', and 'V = πr³' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

A sphere has a radius of 6 cm. What is its surface area? (Use π ≈ 3.14)

The surface area of a sphere is SA = 4πr². Substitute the radius and calculate. The other options — '113.04 cm²', '226.08 cm²', and '904.32 cm²' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is a great circle?

Great Circle A great circle is the intersection of a sphere and a plane through the center. Surface Area of a Sphere S = 4πr² Volume of a Sphere V = (4/3)πr³ Hemisphere Surface area (total) = 3πr² (curved surface + base circle). The other options — 'Any circle drawn on the surface of a sphere', 'The largest circle that fits inside a sphere', and 'The equator of the sphere only' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the volume of a hemisphere with radius r?

The volume of a sphere is V = (4/3)πr³. Substitute the radius and calculate. The other options — '(4/3)πr³', '(1/3)πr³', and '2πr³' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: If the radius of a sphere is tripled, the volume is multiplied by:

The volume of a sphere is V = (4/3)πr³. Substitute the radius and calculate. The other options — '3', '9', and '81' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
